title: User Follow/Unfollow Functionality
description: The system shall allow a logged-in user to follow or unfollow other users and update follower/following counts in real-time or on page refresh.
rationale: This feature is essential for managing social connections and understanding the influence of followed users.
business_value: Improves user engagement and provides insights into the popularity and influence of other users within the platform.

source.description: As a user I need to follow or unfollow other users and see the follower/following counts on their profiles so that I can manage my connections and understand their social influence.

acceptance_criteria: Given I am logged in and viewing another user's profile When I click the 'Follow' button Then the button changes to 'Unfollow' and the follower count on that profile increases by 1 And the user I followed sees a new follower notification (if applicable)
Given I am currently following a user When I click the 'Unfollow' button on their profile Then the button changes to 'Follow' and the follower count on that profile decreases by 1
preconditions: The user must be logged in
Another user's profile page is being viewed
postconditions: If following, the user should see an updated follower count of +1 on their profile and the target user's followers list should include the current user
If unfollowing, the user should see an updated follower count of -1 on their profile and the target user's followers list should no longer include the current user
functional_constraints: Follower and following counts must update in real-time or after a page refresh
The 'Follow' button text changes to 'Unfollow' when followed, and back to 'Follow' when unfollowed
security_requirements: Authentication is required before performing follow/unfollow actions

non_functional_requirements.performance: Response time for following/unfollowing should be less than 2 seconds
non_functional_requirements.availability: The system must be available at least 99.9% of the time
non_functional_requirements.scalability: The system must handle up to 100,000 concurrent users without performance degradation
non_functional_requirements.usability: Error messages should clearly indicate if follow/unfollow actions fail and provide guidance on how to resolve issues

edge_cases: What happens if a user tries to follow themselves?
How does the system handle network latency during follow/unfollow operations?
What if the target user's profile is private or deleted?
dependencies: Database for User Profiles and Relationships

interfaces.name: Follow/Unfollow API
interfaces.description: The system provides an API endpoint to handle follow/unfollow requests.

interfaces.name: User Profile Database
interfaces.description: Interacts with the database to update follower/following counts and manage user relationships.

test_cases.description: Verify that a logged-in user can follow another user.
test_cases.expected_result: The 'Follow' button changes to 'Unfollow', the follower count increases by 1, and the target user receives a notification.

test_cases.description: Verify that a logged-in user can unfollow another user.
test_cases.expected_result: The 'Unfollow' button changes to 'Follow', the follower count decreases by 1, and the target user is no longer in the follower list.

test_cases.description: Verify that a user cannot follow themselves.
test_cases.expected_result: The system should prevent following oneself and display an appropriate error message.
